element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Required for all enemy encounters throughout labyrinth
Player encounters goblin-troll war; goblins become allies after king is spared
Can be used freely on isometric field to dispatch enemies without triggering combat mode
Gold acquisition for merchant purchases; some items cause instant death if misused
Strategic item acquisition using gold; later revealed to be Mordamir in disguise

element_introduction_order.element: Hostile creatures: invisible shades, flying lizards, Will-o'-the-wisps
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive difficulty escalation; varied combat approaches required
Advanced enemy types requiring tactical spell/weapon use

element_introduction_order.element: Dream vision sequences and lore exposition
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Narrative context about ancient dragons, Erinoch, and Mordamir's true nature
Puzzle-solving combined with danger avoidance; multiple instant-death scenarios
Ultimate combat challenge; narrative payoff revealing Mordamir as merchant and antagonist

typical_relief_triggers: Straw bed rest areas with dream sequences
typical_tension_triggers: Combat mode initiation via NPC contact
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant action interspersed with puzzle-solving and exploration; escalating enemy difficulty creates progressive tension over 7-level descent

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to sword combat and basic goblins; Ulindor encountered early as ally; relatively survivable
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy types (shades, flying lizards, Will-o'-the-wisps); puzzle complexity increases; resource management via gold and items becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Colossal creatures (spider, worm, water norfalc); instant-death item consequences; final confrontation with Mordamir as ultimate challenge
notable_spikes: Level 1 opening: optional goblin-Ulindor rescue scenario (Ulindor death prevents Level 2 completion)
Mid-labyrinth: introduction of invisible shades and multiple new creature types simultaneously
Final level: Mordamir reveal and boss battle combining combat mastery with narrative stakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear 7-level progression deeper into dungeon; each level introduces new enemy archetypes and escalates environmental dangers; vertical progression communicates narrative descent toward truth
Touching NPCs triggers combat mode; fireball spells work freely on isometric field but not in forced combat; creates spatial distinction between free action and constrained engagement
Safe spaces for item purchase using accumulated gold; strategically placed to allow player recovery and resource investment; later revealed as Mordamir's trap mechanism
Straw beds trigger dream sequences revealing ancient dragon civilization and Erinoch lore; narrative pacing distributed across gameplay rather than cutscenes; provides mechanical recovery opportunity
